action.hypothesis: JumpReLU local search appears exhausted, and reverting to the fundamentally different plain top-k SAE may provide a cleaner reconstruction-support tradeoff at K=128 under the same strong optimization settings, serving as a robust architecture reset after the failed gated launch attempts.
action.summary: Switch from stalled JumpReLU tuning to a proxy top-k architecture run at K=128 while keeping the strongest validated training settings fixed for a clean architecture comparison.
action.self_review: This satisfies the requirement to try a fundamentally different architecture approach after prolonged stagnation, while avoiding the invalid gated-only overrides that caused recent crashes. The main risk is top-k underperforming JumpReLU, but it is the cleanest valid architecture pivot available without editing code.
notes_to_memory: Recent gated attempts failed at the launcher interface due to invalid env overrides, so a different architecture probe should avoid architecture-specific override keys unless confirmed valid.
Eight non-improving rounds indicate the current JumpReLU search direction is fundamentally stalled.
This round uses the plain top-k SAE as a clean architecture reset under the same best-known training regime to measure whether JumpReLU-specific gating is part of the quality bottleneck.
next_hypotheses: If top-k improves proxy FVU, promote to full and then stabilize the top-k family before revisiting cost or smaller-K tradeoffs.
If top-k is competitive but not better, compare its runtime and stability against JumpReLU to decide whether it is a better base for further architecture work.
If top-k is clearly worse, conclude that a stronger new family is needed and plan a code-backed architecture exploration round rather than more JumpReLU-local parameter sweeps.
